gaps: The statpack's relist-count and CVSG cuts are entirely '(unknown)' buckets, so the two signal cuts the prompt says to anchor on were unusable; populating relist/distribution counts from the docket's DISTRIBUTED entries would directly serve cells like this one

No hold-detection signal in the corpus row (a long gap between distributions with no disposition); I had to infer the hold from raw docket text and fetch the supplemental brief myself to learn the hold target
A conditional grant-rate cut for call-for-response petitions (respondent waived, Court requested response) would be the single most predictive base rate for SCOTUS cert cells
helpful: Provisioned petition/BIO/QP texts were decisive — the split framing and the BIO's vehicle attack came straight from them with no fetching

fedcourts query --era 2020s surfaced sibling petitions granted/denied 2026-06-30 from the same 6/29 conference, which proved this petition survived the cleanup orders list — the strongest single signal in the cell
metrics/statpack.md modern-cert section anchored the prior (~4.9% grant)
notes: Forward-mode web fetch of the docket-linked supplemental brief (identifying the Pung hold) changed the probability by roughly 3x versus snapshot-only reasoning; cheap per-case provisioning of supplemental briefs alongside petition/BIO would capture this systematically.
